title: Playthrough instrumentation and save snapshots
summary: Add deterministic internal playthrough observability without creating a player-facing telemetry or dashboard system.

guardrails: Keep the completed alpha arc intact: do not reopen High Pass density, season three, biome six, broad planner UI, crafting, combat, or inventory expansion during this campaign.
First stabilize feedback, review hygiene, deterministic proof, and player comprehension; content polish must be evidence-led or tightly scoped to existing surfaces.
Preserve the 256x160 handheld readability budget; prefer cuts, visual payoff, and existing seams over new pages or longer copy.
Respect lane ownership: lane 1 state/tooling, lane 2 science/copy, lane 3 spatial/visual, lane 4 route/support/replay. Shared-file edits need handoff notes.
Every main item must leave tests, browser proof, or a clear dated report; every critic item must either clear the lane or queue a narrow follow-up.
Avoid source-control conflicts by advancing each lane on its own dependency chain and using the scout report to name exact files before implementation.
completion_gate: A maintainer can start from named saves and reproduce first-session, mid-season, route-ready, High Pass-ready, and filed states.

lane-1.name: Systems / station / progression / tooling
lane-1.focus: Create debug-only save snapshot helpers and documented localStorage states for first session, station return, season close, High Pass active, and filed High Pass.
lane-1.owns: runtime state, station surfaces, save safety, validation, packaging, and release tooling
lane-1.avoids: authored science copy, biome geometry density, and route prose unless a packet explicitly calls for a systems seam
lane-1.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-2.name: Content / journal / science / copy budgets
lane-2.focus: Define a kid/adult playtest rubric focused on comprehension, emotional memory, and science takeaways rather than feature requests.
lane-2.owns: science-safe content, notebook/atlas language, source-ledger coverage, content-quality tests, and kid-readable synthesis
lane-2.avoids: movement geometry, station state machines, and route controller behavior
lane-2.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

scout_refinement.main_331_target: Add a source-tracked playtest comprehension rubric for kids and adult observers that emphasizes ecosystem understanding, emotional memory, and friction patterns instead of feature requests.
rubric_sections: reading, control, wayfinding, and station/journal friction
non_goals: runtime code
telemetry
analytics
UI
save schema
route logic
station behavior
personal child data collection

lane-3.name: Spatial / traversal / visual-place feel
lane-3.focus: Capture named visual anchors for each major arc place and mark which frames are allowed to change in later packets.
lane-3.owns: world-space readability, traversal beats, environmental silhouettes, screenshot proof, and biome physical memory
lane-3.avoids: route state, copy-heavy notebook changes, station chrome, and save schema changes
lane-3.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

lane-4.name: Route loop / support / replay / full-arc proof
lane-4.focus: Create route-loop instrumentation assertions for active request, support selection, ready-to-file, filed notice, and replay-cleared states.
lane-4.owns: Route v2 behavior, support differentiation, notebook filing loops, route notices, replay windows, and deterministic route smoke tests
lane-4.avoids: new station pages, broad copy rewrites, raw biome geometry, and new route frameworks
lane-4.handoff_rule: If this lane must touch a shared file outside its preferred paths, the scout report must name the reason and queue a dependency or follow-up for the owning lane.

collision_rules: Lane 1 may touch large coordinator and station files; other lanes should not edit those files in the same packet unless a scout report queues the dependency.
Lane 2 may touch authored content and copy; lane 4 should not rewrite lane-2 prose while route behavior is in flight.
Lane 3 may touch physical placement and visual proof; it should not add route objectives or journal copy as part of geometry work.
Lane 4 may touch route/support/replay behavior; it should not create new station pages, broad content facts, or unrelated geometry.
If two lanes need the same mixed content module, the earlier scout must either sequence the edits or ask lane 1 for a data split before both mains proceed.
proof_expectations: At least one automated test, browser proof, or dated report for every main item.
Critic must record whether handheld readability, source accuracy, and lane ownership stayed intact.
Any follow-up must be narrow, named, and assigned to the owning lane rather than left as general polish.

main_330_focus.target: Add debug-only named save snapshot helpers plus a source-tracked localStorage reference so reviewers can jump to first-session, station-return, season-close, High Pass active, High Pass ready-to-file, and filed High Pass states without replaying the full arc manually.
recommended_state_contract: Export the localStorage key from `src/engine/save.ts` instead of duplicating `eco-explorer-save-v1` in docs or tests.
Keep snapshots as plain `SaveState` JSON and serialize them as `localStorage.setItem(SAVE_STORAGE_KEY, JSON.stringify(snapshot.save))` payloads.

`station-return` should use the existing forest-study completion recipe from `guided-field-season.test.ts` / `field-season-board.test.ts`, including the forest survey discoveries needed for the station-return beat.
`season-close-return` should set `completedFieldRequestIds` to at least `forest-expedition-upper-run` and `forest-season-threads` plus `seasonCloseReturnPending: true`.
`high-pass-active` should use the existing High Pass completion recipe from `runtime-smoke.test.ts` with `seasonCloseReturnPending: false` and no `treeline-high-pass` completion.

`high-pass-ready-to-file` should use `routeV2Progress.requestId: treeline-high-pass`, `status: ready-to-synthesize`, and ordered slots `stone-lift`, `lee-watch`, `rime-mark`, `talus-hold` using `frost-heave-boulder`, `hoary-marmot`, `moss-campion`, and `talus-cushion-pocket`.
verification_targets: the snapshot tests round-trip every payload through `normalizeSaveState`
the snapshot tests assert the expected guided-season, field-station, or High Pass chapter phase for each named state
`npm run validate:agents` passes after queue and packet updates
non_goals: Do not add telemetry, analytics, network calls, a player-facing snapshot menu, or a debug dashboard.
Do not add a save-schema migration or change persisted field meanings beyond exporting the existing storage key if needed.
Do not change route behavior, station layout, route copy, biome geometry, science content, or support behavior.
Do not build packet `133`'s full deterministic route-state matrix or duplicate lane-4 route-loop assertions.

main_332_focus.target: Extend the source-tracked screenshot manifest with an anchor-change policy so every lane-3 visual anchor says whether it is a protected baseline, expected recapture, or state gap.
snapshot_contract: Fresh browser errors use `output/alpha-screenshot-proof/<frame-id>-errors.json` when available.
Generated output remains ignored and must not be committed.
verification_targets: `npm run validate:agents` passes after queue and packet updates
all 15 frame ids have an anchor class and allowed-change or recapture trigger
older references without adjacent state JSON are marked as state gaps
non_goals: Do not add runtime code, telemetry, analytics, save helpers, route assertions, or browser automation.
Do not generate or commit screenshot files.
Do not change biome geometry, traversal behavior, route state, station UI, save schema, or science copy.
Do not duplicate lane 1's save snapshot documentation or lane 4's route-loop assertions.

main_333_focus.target: Tighten lane-4 route-loop instrumentation assertions around the existing High Pass runtime smoke so `render_game_to_text()` proves active request, support choice, ready-to-file, filed notice, and replay-cleared states without adding telemetry or a new debug dashboard.
debug_checkpoints: active `treeline-high-pass` state exposes `activeFieldRequest`, `nearestInspectableEntityId`, and the `support-biased` `fieldRequestHint` for `hand-lens`
non-`hand-lens` support on the same pocket keeps the hint `default` and does not retarget to the notebook-fit talus clue
after talus-hold inspect, `routeV2Progress.status` is `ready-to-synthesize`, `activeFieldRequest.progressLabel` is `Ready To File`, and the notice variant is `notebook-ready`
station-side filing clears `routeV2Progress`, records `treeline-high-pass`, and emits the route-authored `HIGH PASS` filed notice with `filed-route` variant
post-filed world-map and journal states expose no active request, route marker, replay label, or journal field request
verification_targets: `npm test -- src/test/runtime-smoke.test.ts -t "High Pass"` or a narrower equivalent focused on the edited High Pass route-loop assertions passes
`npm run validate:agents` passes after queue and packet updates
non_goals: Do not add runtime telemetry, analytics, player-facing UI, screenshots, save schema, or a dashboard.
Do not change Route v2 behavior, support selection behavior, station layout, route copy, biome geometry, or science content.
Do not build packet `133`'s full deterministic route-state matrix.
Do not duplicate lane-1 save snapshot helpers or lane-3 visual-anchor capture policy.
